segments.name: Capital Markets (Broking + IB)
segments.description: Equity, derivatives, commodity, currency trading + institutional services and investment banking. Brokerage now contributes only 30% of total revenue, down from historical highs.

segments.name: Asset Management
segments.description: Mutual funds and PMS. 91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years.

segments.name: Private Wealth Management
segments.description: HNI/UHNI advisory. Family count up 41% YoY.

segments.name: Housing Finance
segments.description: Retail mortgage lending — affordable and mid-segment.

valuation_bands.notes: 28x P/E on FY27E EPS of INR 50; ARR > 70%, AMC +40% YoY.
24x P/E on FY27E EPS of INR 47; ARR stable at 65%, steady AUM.
18x P/E on depressed FY27E EPS of INR 35; sustained regulatory headwinds.

scenarios.name: Bull
scenarios.notes: ARR > 70%, AMC AUM +40% YoY, regulatory clarity.

scenarios.name: Base
scenarios.notes: ARR steady at 65%, AUM compounding, stable regulation.

scenarios.name: Bear
scenarios.notes: Sustained F&O headwinds, fintech share loss.

dimensions.name: Scalability of Core Engine
dimensions.assessment: ARR at 61% of net revenues; fee-based at 45%. H1FY26 operating PAT INR 1,088 Cr (+11% YoY) on net revenue INR 2,888 Cr (+15%). Asset-light fee model scales without proportional cost growth. F&O constraints limit broking scalability near-term.
dimensions.key_evidence: AMC AUM +33% YoY to INR 1.89L Cr with INR 11,610 Cr net flows.

dimensions.name: Addressable Market & Adjacency
dimensions.assessment: 16.57L HNWIs by 2027; India 4th-largest private wealth market by 2028 (+58.4% UHNWIs). Sector profits to nearly double by FY30. MF penetration at 5–6%. Massive TAM with adjacency expansion across capital markets, wealth, housing, alternatives.
dimensions.key_evidence: India private credit hit USD 9.0 Bn in H1 2025 (+53% YoY).

dimensions.name: Asymmetric Pricing Power
dimensions.assessment: 91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years gives pricing power in PMS/wealth. Brokerage revenue down 24% from regulatory changes — fee compression in cyclical segment. Discount brokers and fintech limit pricing in commoditized broking.
dimensions.key_evidence: Diversified mix offset 24% brokerage decline, validating partial pricing-power hedge.

dimensions.name: Reinvestment Quality
dimensions.assessment: MCap INR 49,079 Cr (+24.8% YoY); P/E 24.2; P/B 3.82. Strong RoCE; disciplined allocation into high-return fee businesses. Reinvestment in tech, talent, distribution, research generates sustainable advantage.
dimensions.key_evidence: PWM AUM +31% YoY to INR 1.96L Cr; family count +41%; PAT +14% (9M).

dimensions.name: Track Record Through Adversity
dimensions.assessment: Q2FY25 PAT INR 1,242 Cr (+123% YoY), 46% revenue growth, 48% RoE — recovery and execution. Q2FY26 PAT -67.6% YoY, revenue -34.9% YoY shows residual cyclicality from F&O regulation. Continuity across cycles maintained.
dimensions.key_evidence: Despite repeated SEBI interventions, AUM and ARR base continue to compound — institutional resilience.

dimensions.name: Human Capital & Institutional DNA
dimensions.assessment: Raamdeo Agrawal: QGLP creator, 'Buy Right, Sit Tight' framework. Navin Agarwal (since 2000): business strategy and capital allocation. Ajay Menon (27+ yrs): operations, compliance, risk, technology. Founding duo across nearly four decades — rare DNA in Indian financial services.
dimensions.key_evidence: Rashtriya Samman Patra recipient (top tax-payer FY95–FY99) signals early ethical commercial success.

dimensions.name: Inflection Point Identification
dimensions.assessment: Consensus TP INR 1,126.67 (max INR 1,200) vs CMP INR 822.90 — 37% upside; market not yet pricing ARR re-rating. Credit rating upgraded to AA+ (Stable) by ICRA. ARR > 70% would trigger re-rating from broking to asset-management multiples within 6–18 months.
dimensions.key_evidence: ARR at 65% of net revenues (up from 61%); management guiding higher — measurable inflection.

management_team.name: Raamdeo Agrawal
management_team.bio: Co-founder (1987). Creator of the QGLP investment process and 'Buy Right, Sit Tight' framework. Built one of India's most respected investment research franchises. Widely cited investment thinker.

management_team.name: Motilal Oswal
management_team.bio: Co-founder (1987). Led the firm's transformation from broking house into a comprehensive financial services platform. Recipient of Rashtriya Samman Patra for being among the highest taxpayers FY95–FY99.

management_team.name: Navin Agarwal
management_team.bio: Joined 2000. Drives business strategy and capital allocation across the Group, building market-leading positions through disciplined deployment.

management_team.name: Ajay Menon
management_team.bio: Joined 1998. 27+ years in Indian capital markets. Cornerstone of operational excellence and technology initiatives across the Group.

forensic.overall_assessment: Recurring regulatory matters are operational rather than fraudulent. Despite SEBI interventions, structural lapses suggest deeper governance attention needed in risk management. Pattern warrants monitoring but does not impair the business model.

violations.title: SEBI — Margin Reporting Violations
violations.description: INR 7 lakh penalty for margin reporting, collection violations, and other regulatory lapses found during inspection.

violations.title: SEBI — Client Fund Misutilization (2022)
violations.description: INR 25 lakh fine for misutilization of client funds and inaccurate margin reporting.

violations.title: NSEL — Misselling Accusations
violations.description: Accused alongside other top brokers of misselling NSEL contracts, KYC manipulation, client code modification, and illegal transactions per SFIO/EOW findings.

violations.title: SEBI — Not Fit and Proper (Commodities)
violations.description: Declared 'not fit and proper' as commodity derivative broker in Feb 2019 based on SFIO/EOW recommendations.

violations.title: Unauthorized Terminal Operations
violations.description: 13 NSE and 9 BSE terminals operating from undeclared locations including an unreported branch in Delhi.

key_highlights.title: ARR Transformation
key_highlights.body: ARR at 65% of net revenues (up from 61%); fee-based at 45%. Annuity income reduces transaction-cycle volatility.

key_highlights.title: Profitability Momentum
key_highlights.body: Q2FY25 PAT INR 1,242 Cr (+123% YoY); H1FY26 operating PAT INR 1,088 Cr (+11% YoY); Asset & PWM businesses leading at +32% PAT growth.

key_highlights.title: AUM Scale and Outperformance
key_highlights.body: Total AUM INR 1.77L Cr (+46% YoY). AMC AUM INR 1.89L Cr (+33%) with INR 11,610 Cr net flows. 91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years.

key_highlights.title: Private Wealth Expansion
key_highlights.body: PWM AUM INR 1.96L Cr (+31% YoY); family count +41% YoY; PAT +14% (9M). Distribution book +34% YoY to INR 42,775 Cr.

key_highlights.title: Valuation Opportunity
key_highlights.body: CMP INR 822.90 vs consensus TP INR 1,126.67 (max INR 1,200) — 37% upside. Probability-weighted target INR 1,109.

key_highlights.title: Credit Upgrade
key_highlights.body: Long-term credit rating upgraded to AA+ (Stable) by ICRA. Net worth INR 12,871 Cr (+16% YoY).

competitive_advantages: Research franchise covering 300+ companies — institutional moat
QGLP investment philosophy with 'Buy Right, Sit Tight' framework
65% ARR contribution — structural revenue-quality improvement vs broking peers
91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years
550+ cities and 2,500+ business locations — deep distribution
Integrated platform: broking → PMS → wealth → IB cross-sell
AA+ (Stable) credit rating — balance sheet strength
Founder-led vision with institutional depth across four decades
Alternative investment capability with recurring carry income
Technology investment in digital advisory and client servicing
industry_tailwinds: Mutual fund penetration at 5-6% — massive latent opportunity
Financial savings shifting from gold/real estate to financial assets
Private wealth set to be 4th-largest globally by 2028
NBFCs growing at 16% annually with improving asset quality
Demographic dividend — large working-age population entering peak earning
Digital payments maturing — new distribution channels
industry_risks: Regulatory uncertainty on capital adequacy, risk, consumer protection
Fintech disruption — 14,000+ startups offering lower-cost alternatives
Global risk aversion driving FII outflows and capital-market slowdowns
Reliance on foreign capital creates external sector vulnerability

trading_strategy.entry_rationale: 15-20% discount to analyst consensus target — margin of safety.
review_metrics: ARR contribution above 60% of net revenues
AMC AUM growth above 25% YoY with net flows > INR 10,000 Cr
PWM AUM growth above 25% YoY with family count expansion
SEBI compliance with no major enforcement actions
upside_exit: Sell remaining at INR 1,200+ if multiples expand beyond 25x P/E
trading_strategy.downside_exit: Stop-loss at INR 750 (-8% from CMP) on technical break with high volume
thesis_breaking_exits: ARR contribution drops below 55% for two consecutive quarters
Major SEBI enforcement action with direct business impact
AMC AUM declining for two consecutive quarters
Management guidance signaling structural headwinds beyond FY26
Competitive market-share loss in core wealth or AMC segments
